judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 2 is stronger overall: both reports match on grouping/ordering and flagging F-06 as a policy decision, but Report 2 clearly recommends overriding RESULTS_DB to prevent parallel test collisions while Report 1 hedges that the DB "isn't currently read by any test," undercutting that expectation.

report_1.evidence: Group B — `pipeline/registry.py` (one engineer): F-02 (missing `aurora-1` endpoint), F-05 (`DEFAULT_RUBRIC_VERSION` stale), F-09 (`DEFAULT_DATASET` points at a deleted file). All three are independent one-line constant fixes in the same small file (lines 3-11) — bundling them avoids a 3-way merge on adjacent lines

report_2.evidence: Engineer A — `pipeline/registry.py`** (F-02 add `aurora-1` endpoint mapping, F-05 bump `DEFAULT_RUBRIC_VERSION` to `rubric-v3`, F-09 fix `DEFAULT_DATASET` path). Three independent constants in one small file; trivial, no logic branching, safe as one owner doing three small commits.

report_1.evidence: Ordering: Groups B and D have zero dependencies and can start immediately, in parallel, by anyone. Group A should start immediately too, but as a single owner working the whole file rather than split further, and Group C's second half (F-11) should not start until Group C's first half (F-01) lands

report_2.evidence: Engineers A, B, C, and D can all start in **wave 1, in parallel** — their target files (`registry.py`, `rubric.py`+new test file, `run_batch.py`, the two prompt files) are fully disjoint... Engineer B — ... (F-01 fix multi-line criteria truncation, F-11 add the edge-case parsing test).

report_1.evidence: F-06 ("tighten thresholds so borderline answers score lower") has no target values... This should go back to whoever owns the grading rubric/quality bar for a target value before it becomes an engineering task.

report_2.evidence: F-06 is a product decision dressed as a bug fix.** ... that's a behavior change needing sign-off from whoever owns the rubric/scoring policy, not an engineering judgment call. Escalate for a decision on the actual target thresholds before it's implemented.

report_1.evidence: Awareness of `tests/conftest.py:4` — `RESULTS_DB` defaults to `/var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db`, which does not exist on this machine (confirmed via `ls`). It isn't currently read by any test, but anyone adding F-11/F-12 tests should check whether new fixtures start depending on it, and set the `RESULTS_DB` env var or create the directory if so.

report_2.evidence: Awareness that `RESULTS_DB` (`tests/conftest.py`) defaults to `/var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db` — if any test path actually touches that DB (none of the current fixes appear to, but the fixture is imported), engineers running test suites in parallel should override `RESULTS_DB` to their own scratch path so parallel runs don't collide, per project documentation's "own isolated test resource" guidance.
